---
title: "Data Visualization"
author: "yahia abulibdeh"
date: "2025-11-26"
output:
  word_document: default
  pdf_document: default
  html_document: default
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

```


```{r, include=FALSE}
options(tinytex.verbose = TRUE)
```

```{r Q1.Do people with different levels of physical activity differ in their resilience (BRS) and stress mindset (SMM) scores?}

library(ggplot2)
library(writexl)
library(readxl)
library(reshape2)
library(dplyr)
library(corrplot)

# Read the data and make exploratory and preprocessing

df <- read_excel(file.choose())
                                                                              

df <- df[!is.na(df$`Are you currently enrolled in college?`), ]



# Question _1_resilience_StressMindset_PhysicalActivity
q1_rs_stmd_ac <- df[, c("1. I tend to bounce back quickly after hard times",
                  "2. I have a hard time making it through stressful events",
                  "3. It does not take me long to recover from a stressful event.",
                  "4. It is hard for me to snap back when something bad happens.",
                  "5. I usually come through difficult times with little trouble.",
                  "6. I tend to take a long time to get over set-backs in my life.",
                  "The effects of stress are negative and should be avoided.",
                  "Experiencing stress facilitates my learning and growth.",
                  "Experiencing stress depletes my health and vitality.",
                  "Experiencing stress enhances my performance and productivity.",
                  "Experiencing stress inhibits my learning and growth.",
                  "Experiencing stress improves my health and vitality.",
                  "Experiencing stress debilitates my performance and productivity.",
                  "The effects of stress are positive and should be utilized.",
                  "How many times per week, on average, do you engage in physical activity for at least 20 minutes a day?")]


# Find missing values

cat("The missing values in the columns:")

colSums(is.na(q1_rs_stmd_ac))


# Preprocessing ...

# Take the numraic columns and fill the nan values with mean

numaric_col <- sapply(q1_rs_stmd_ac, is.numeric)

q1_rs_stmd_ac[numaric_col] <- lapply(q1_rs_stmd_ac[numaric_col], function(x){
  x[is.na(x)] <-median(x, na.rm = TRUE)
  x <- as.integer(round(x))
  return(x)
})

# Reverse the columns that are reverse scored which they are the 2, 4, 6 columns in resilience   columns

cols_to_reverse <- c(
  "2. I have a hard time making it through stressful events",
  "4. It is hard for me to snap back when something bad happens.",
  "6. I tend to take a long time to get over set-backs in my life."
)

q1_rs_stmd_ac[cols_to_reverse] <- lapply(q1_rs_stmd_ac[cols_to_reverse], function(x) {
  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    return(ifelse(x == 1, 5,
           ifelse(x == 2, 4,
           ifelse(x == 3, 3,
           ifelse(x == 4, 2,
           ifelse(x == 5, 1, x))))))
  } else {
    warning("Non-numeric column detected and skipped: ", deparse(substitute(x)))
    return(x)
  }
})

# Make function for pre process to process the nan values in categorical columns

get_mode <- function(x){
  ux <- unique(x)
  ux[which.max(tabulate(match(x, ux)))]
}

# Process the nan values

catigorical_col <- sapply(q1_rs_stmd_ac, is.character)

q1_rs_stmd_ac[catigorical_col] <- lapply(q1_rs_stmd_ac[catigorical_col], function(x){
  x[is.na(x)] <- get_mode(x)
  return(x)
})


# Turn the categorical columns to numeric to interpret the result in stress mindset columns

q1_rs_stmd_ac$`The effects of stress are negative and should be avoided.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`The effects of stress are negative and should be avoided.`,
         "Strongly Disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor Disagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)


q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress facilitates my learning and growth.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress facilitates my learning and growth.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor Disagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)

q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress depletes my health and vitality.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress depletes my health and vitality.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)

q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress enhances my performance and productivity.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress enhances my performance and productivity.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)

q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress inhibits my learning and growth.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress inhibits my learning and growth.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)

q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress improves my health and vitality.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress improves my health and vitality.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)

q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress debilitates my performance and productivity.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Experiencing stress debilitates my performance and productivity.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)

q1_rs_stmd_ac$`The effects of stress are positive and should be utilized.` <-
  recode(q1_rs_stmd_ac$`The effects of stress are positive and should be utilized.`,
         "Strongly Disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor Disagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)

# Create a new column to interpret the resilience score (BRS_Score)

rs_columns <- c(
  "1. I tend to bounce back quickly after hard times",
  "2. I have a hard time making it through stressful events",
  "3. It does not take me long to recover from a stressful event.",
  "4. It is hard for me to snap back when something bad happens.",
  "5. I usually come through difficult times with little trouble.",
  "6. I tend to take a long time to get over set-backs in my life."
)

q1_rs_stmd_ac$BRS_Score <- rowSums(q1_rs_stmd_ac[rs_columns], na.rm = TRUE)


# Create a new column to categorise the BRS_Score

q1_rs_stmd_ac <- q1_rs_stmd_ac %>%
  mutate(BRS_Score_Interpretation = case_when(
    BRS_Score >= 6  & BRS_Score <= 13 ~ "Low resilience",
    BRS_Score >= 14 & BRS_Score <= 21 ~ "Normal resilience",
    BRS_Score >= 22 & BRS_Score <= 30 ~ "High resilience",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

 
# Create a new column to interpret the stress mindset score

stmd_columns <- c("The effects of stress are negative and should be avoided.",
                  "Experiencing stress facilitates my learning and growth.",
                  "Experiencing stress depletes my health and vitality.",
                  "Experiencing stress enhances my performance and productivity.",
                  "Experiencing stress inhibits my learning and growth.",
                  "Experiencing stress improves my health and vitality.",
                  "Experiencing stress debilitates my performance and productivity.",
                  "The effects of stress are positive and should be utilized.")


# Create a new column to interpret the stress Mindset score (SMM) 

q1_rs_stmd_ac$SMM <- rowSums(q1_rs_stmd_ac[stmd_columns], na.rm = TRUE)


# Create a new column to categorise the SMM


q1_rs_stmd_ac <- q1_rs_stmd_ac %>%
  mutate(SMM_Interpretation = case_when(
    SMM >= 0  & SMM <= 10 ~ "Stress-is-Debilitating Mindset",
    SMM >= 11 & SMM <= 21 ~ "Neutral/Mixed Mindset",
    SMM >= 22 & SMM <= 32 ~ "Stress-is-Enhancing Mindset",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))



cat("The missing values in the columns after pre process:")

colSums(is.na(q1_rs_stmd_ac))

q1_rs_stmd_ac <- q1_rs_stmd_ac %>%
  rename(Physical_Activity = `How many times per week, on average, do you engage in physical activity for at least 20 minutes a day?`)

activity_levels <- c(
  "None" = 0,
  "1 time" = 1,
  "2 times" = 2,
  "3 times" = 3,
  "4 times" = 4,
  "5 times" = 5,
  "6-7 times" = 6
)



q1_rs_stmd_ac$activity_ordinal <- activity_levels[q1_rs_stmd_ac$`Physical_Activity`]

```


```{r Q1.~visualisations}

q1_rs_stmd_ac$Physical_Activity <- factor(q1_rs_stmd_ac$Physical_Activity,
                           levels = c("None", "1 time", "2 times", "3 times", "4 times", "5 times", "6-7 times"))

plot1 <- ggplot(q1_rs_stmd_ac, aes(x = BRS_Score, y = SMM)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = BRS_Score_Interpretation), size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, linewidth = 1.2, color = "darkblue", fill = "lightblue", alpha = 0.3) +
  facet_wrap(~ Physical_Activity, ncol = 3) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Low resilience" = "#e74c3c", "Normal resilience" = "#f39c12", "High resilience" = "#27ae60")) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Resilience and Stress Mindset",
    subtitle = "Stratified by Weekly Physical Activity Frequency",
    x = "Brief Resilience Scale (BRS) Score (Higher = More Resilient)",
    y = "Stress Mindset Measure (SMM) Score (Higher = More Stressful Mindset)",
    color = "Resilience Level"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40"),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "gray90", color = "gray80"),
    panel.spacing = unit(1, "lines"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

plot1

ggsave("Fig1_Resilience_Stress_by_Activity.png", plot = plot1, width = 12, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

plot2 <- ggplot(q1_rs_stmd_ac, aes(x = BRS_Score, y = SMM)) +
  geom_point(aes(color = BRS_Score_Interpretation, size = SMM), alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "darkred", fill = "pink") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Low resilience" = "#e74c3c", "Normal resilience" = "#f39c12", "High resilience" = "#27ae60")) +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(1, 4)) +
  labs(
    title = "Resilience vs Stress Mindset Relationship",
    subtitle = "Bubble size represents stress mindset intensity",
    x = "Resilience Score (BRS)",
    y = "Stress Mindset Score (SMM)",
    color = "Resilience Level",
    size = "Stress Level"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40")
  )

plot2

ggsave("Fig2_Resilience_Stress_Bubble.png", plot = plot2, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

q1_rs_stmd_ac$Physical_Activity<- factor(q1_rs_stmd_ac$Physical_Activity,
                         levels = c("None", "1 time", "2 times", "3 times", "4 times", "5 times", "6-7 times"))

plot3 <- ggplot(q1_rs_stmd_ac, aes(x = Physical_Activity, y = SMM, fill = Physical_Activity)) +
  geom_boxplot(
    alpha = 0.8,
    color = "gray30",
    linewidth = 0.5,
    outlier.shape = 21,
    outlier.fill = "#e74c3c",
    outlier.color = "gray30",
    outlier.size = 2
  ) +
  stat_summary(
    fun = median,
    geom = "point",
    shape = 21,
    size = 3,
    fill = "white",
    color = "gray30"
  ) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "plasma", alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Stress Mindset Scores Across Physical Activity Levels",
    subtitle = "Higher SMM scores indicate more stressful mindset",
    x = "Weekly Physical Activity Frequency",
    y = "Stress Mindset Score (SMM)",
    caption = "Median values shown with white dots"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 15)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, color = "gray50", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1, size = 10),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray90", linewidth = 0.3),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 32, by = 4))

plot3

ggsave("Fig3_SMM_by_Activity_Boxplot.png", plot = plot3, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")


plot4 <- ggplot(q1_rs_stmd_ac, aes(x = activity_ordinal, y = SMM)) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = SMM),
    width = 0.15,
    height = 0,
    size = 3,
    alpha = 0.6,
    shape = 16
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE,
    color = "#2c3e50",
    fill = "#3498db",
    alpha = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  scale_color_gradient2(
    low = "#2ecc71",
    mid = "#f1c40f",
    high = "#e74c3c",
    midpoint = 16,
    name = "SMM Score"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Physical Activity and Stress Mindset",
    subtitle = "Linear trend shows the association between activity frequency and perceived stress",
    x = "Physical Activity Frequency (Ordinal Scale: 0-6)",
    y = "Stress Mindset Score (SMM)",
    caption = paste("Spearman correlation:", round(cor(q1_rs_stmd_ac$activity_ordinal, q1_rs_stmd_ac$SMM, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"), 3))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 15)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray50", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray92", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray96", linewidth = 0.2),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:6, labels = c("None", "1 time", "2 times", "3 times", "4 times", "5 times", "6-7 times"))

plot4

ggsave("Fig4_Activity_vs_SMM_Jitter.png", plot = plot4, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

plot5 <- ggplot(q1_rs_stmd_ac, aes(x = activity_ordinal, y = BRS_Score)) +
  geom_jitter(
    aes(color = BRS_Score_Interpretation),
    width = 0.15,
    height = 0,
    size = 3.5,
    alpha = 0.7,
    shape = 19
  ) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    se = TRUE,
    color = "#27ae60",
    fill = "#2ecc71",
    alpha = 0.2,
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Low resilience" = "#e74c3c", "Normal resilience" = "#f39c12", "High resilience" = "#27ae60")) +
  labs(
    title = "Relationship Between Physical Activity and Resilience",
    subtitle = "Positive trend suggests physical activity may enhance psychological resilience",
    x = "Physical Activity Frequency",
    y = "Resilience Score (BRS)",
    color = "Resilience Level",
    caption = paste("Spearman correlation:", round(cor(q1_rs_stmd_ac$activity_ordinal, q1_rs_stmd_ac$BRS_Score, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs"), 3))
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 15)),
    plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray50", margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    panel.grid.major = element_line(color = "gray92", linewidth = 0.4),
    panel.grid.minor = element_line(color = "gray96", linewidth = 0.2),
    plot.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA),
    panel.background = element_rect(fill = "white", color = NA)
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 0:6, labels = c("None", "1 time", "2 times", "3 times", "4 times", "5 times", "6-7 times"), limits = c(-0.5, 6.5)) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(6, 30, by = 4))

plot5

ggsave("Fig5_Activity_vs_Resilience_Jitter.png", plot = plot5, width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

df_corr <- q1_rs_stmd_ac[, c("SMM", "BRS_Score")]
corr_matrix <- cor(df_corr, method = "spearman", use = "complete.obs")

corrplot(corr_matrix, 
         method = "color",
         type = "upper",
         order = "hclust",
         tl.col = "black",
         tl.srt = 45,
         col = colorRampPalette(c("#e74c3c", "#ecf0f1", "#3498db"))(100),
         addCoef.col = "white",
         number.cex = 1,
         tl.cex = 0.9,
         cl.lim = c(-1, 1),
         title = "Correlation Matrix: Stress Mindset and Resilience",
         mar = c(0, 0, 2, 0),
         cl.offset = 0.5)

plot_record <- recordPlot()

png("Fig6_correlation_matrix.png", width = 1000, height = 800, res = 150)
replayPlot(plot_record)
dev.off()
```




```{r Q1.~Aplly the tests}

# Check the normality
shapiro.test(q1_rs_stmd_ac$SMM)

lm_q1_smm <- lm(SMM ~ Physical_Activity, data = q1_rs_stmd_ac)
summary(lm_q1_smm)

lm_q1_rs <- lm(BRS_Score ~ Physical_Activity, data = q1_rs_stmd_ac)
summary(lm_q1_rs)

cor.test(q1_rs_stmd_ac$SMM, q1_rs_stmd_ac$BRS_Score, method = "spearman")
```




```{r Q2.Does the strees mindest affected by gender?}

# Select the columns to answer the question

q2_stmd <- df[, c("What is your gender?",
                  "The effects of stress are negative and should be avoided.",
                  "Experiencing stress facilitates my learning and growth.",
                  "Experiencing stress depletes my health and vitality.",
                  "Experiencing stress enhances my performance and productivity.",
                  "Experiencing stress inhibits my learning and growth.",
                  "Experiencing stress improves my health and vitality.",
                  "Experiencing stress debilitates my performance and productivity.",
                  "The effects of stress are positive and should be utilized.")]

cat("The missing values in the columns:")

colSums(is.na(q2_stmd))


# Fill nan values

catigorical_col <- sapply(q2_stmd, is.character)
q2_stmd[catigorical_col] <- lapply(q2_stmd[catigorical_col], function(x){
  x[is.na(x)] <- get_mode(x)
  return(x)
})

 
# Turn the categorical columns to numeric to interpret the result 

q2_stmd$`The effects of stress are negative and should be avoided.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`The effects of stress are negative and should be avoided.`,
         "Strongly Disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor Disagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)


q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress facilitates my learning and growth.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress facilitates my learning and growth.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor Disagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)

q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress depletes my health and vitality.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress depletes my health and vitality.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)

q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress enhances my performance and productivity.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress enhances my performance and productivity.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)

q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress inhibits my learning and growth.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress inhibits my learning and growth.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)

q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress improves my health and vitality.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress improves my health and vitality.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)

q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress debilitates my performance and productivity.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`Experiencing stress debilitates my performance and productivity.`,
         "Strongly disagree" = 4,
         "Disagree" = 3,
         "Neither Agree nor DIsagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 1,
         "Strongly Agree" = 0)

q2_stmd$`The effects of stress are positive and should be utilized.` <-
  recode(q2_stmd$`The effects of stress are positive and should be utilized.`,
         "Strongly Disagree" = 0,
         "Disagree" = 1,
         "Neither Agree nor Disagree" = 2,
         "Agree" = 3,
         "Strongly Agree" = 4)


# Create a new column to interpret the stress Mindset score (SMM) 


q2_stmd$SMM <- rowSums(q2_stmd[sapply(q2_stmd, is.numeric)])


# Create a new column to categorise the SMM

q2_stmd <- q2_stmd %>%
  mutate(SMM_Interpretation = case_when(
    SMM >= 0  & SMM <= 10 ~ "Stress-is-Debilitating Mindset",
    SMM >= 11 & SMM <= 21 ~ "Neutral/Mixed Mindset",
    SMM >= 22 & SMM <= 32 ~ "Stress-is-Enhancing Mindset",
    TRUE ~ NA_character_
  ))

# Check the data after pre process

cat("The missing values in the columns after pre process:")

colSums(is.na(q2_stmd))

```


```{r Q2.~visualisations}

q2_stmd_p1<- ggplot(q2_stmd, aes(x = SMM, fill = `What is your gender?`)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.6) +
  labs(title = "SMM Density by Gender in Each Interpretation Category") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14)

q2_stmd_p1
ggsave("q2_stmd_p1.png", q2_stmd_p1, 
       width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

q2_stmd_p2 <- ggplot(q2_stmd, aes(x = SMM, fill = `What is your gender?`)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.7, color = NA) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = c(10.5, 21.5), linetype = "dashed", color = "gray40", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ `What is your gender?`, ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Male" = "#2E86AB", "Female" = "#A23B72")) +
  labs(title = "Stress Mindset Distribution by Gender",
       subtitle = "Dashed lines show mindset boundaries: Debilitating (0-10), Neutral (11-21), Enhancing (22-32)",
       x = "Stress Mindset Measure (SMM) Score", 
       y = "Density",
       fill = "Gender") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40", margin = margin(b = 15)),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    legend.position = "none",
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.spacing = unit(1.5, "lines")
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(0, 32, by = 5))


q2_stmd_p2

ggsave("q2_stmd_p2.png", q2_stmd_p2, 
       width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white") 


q2_stmd_p3 <- ggplot(q2_stmd) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(y = SMM, x = `What is your gender?`, fill = `What is your gender?`), color= "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Male" = "#2E86AB",
    "Female" = "pink"
  ))+
  labs(
    x = "Gender",
    y = "SMM",
    title = "SMM scores by gender"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16),
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1)
  )
ggsave("q2_stmd_p3.png", q2_stmd_p3, 
       width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")

q2_stmd_p3

# Create a factor with levels ordered by median SMM score in descending order
q2_stmd <- q2_stmd %>%
  mutate(SMM_Interpretation = factor(
    SMM_Interpretation,
    levels = c(
      "Stress-is-Enhancing Mindset",
      "Neutral/Mixed Mindset", 
      "Stress-is-Debilitating Mindset"
    )
  ))

q2_stmd_p4 <- ggplot(q2_stmd) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(y = SMM, x = `What is your gender?`, fill = SMM_Interpretation), 
               color = "black", alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Stress-is-Debilitating Mindset" = "#2ecc71", 
                              "Neutral/Mixed Mindset" = "#f39c12", 
                              "Stress-is-Enhancing Mindset" = "#e74c3c")) +
  labs(
    x = "Gender",
    y = "Stress Mindset Measure (SMM) Score",
    title = "Stress Mindset Scores by Gender and Stress Level",
    subtitle = "Colored by stress interpretation categories (ordered descending by mindset)",
    fill = "Stress Level"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0.5, color = "gray40"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    legend.position = "bottom"
  )

q2_stmd_p4

ggsave("q2_stmd_p4.png", q2_stmd_p4, 
       width = 9, height = 7, dpi = 300, bg = "white")


```

```{r Apply the test}

# Check normality
shapiro.test(q2_stmd$SMM)

wilcox.test(SMM ~ `What is your gender?`, data = q2_stmd)

# save the data as Excel
write.csv(q2_stmd, "q2_gender_stress_data.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r}

new_df <- q1_rs_stmd_ac

new_df$Gender <- q2_stmd$`What is your gender?`

write.csv(
  new_df,
  "clean_dataset_for_q1_q2.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)

```




```{r}


smm_stats <- q2_stmd %>%
  summarise(
    Variable = "Stress Mindset (SMM)",
    Mean = round(mean(SMM, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD = round(sd(SMM, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Median = median(SMM, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min = min(SMM, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(SMM, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )

brs_stats <- q1_rs_stmd_ac %>%
  summarise(
    Variable = "Resilience (BRS)",
    Mean = round(mean(BRS_Score, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SD = round(sd(BRS_Score, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    Median = median(BRS_Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    Min = min(BRS_Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = max(BRS_Score, na.rm = TRUE),
    n = n()
  )


desc_stats <- bind_rows(smm_stats, brs_stats)
print(desc_stats)

resilience_freq <- q1_rs_stmd_ac %>%
  count(BRS_Score_Interpretation) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(n/sum(n)*100, 1))

stress_freq <- q2_stmd %>%
  count(SMM_Interpretation) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(n/sum(n)*100, 1))

gender_freq <- q2_stmd %>%
  count(`What is your gender?`) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(n/sum(n)*100, 1))

print(resilience_freq)
print(stress_freq)
print(gender_freq)

activity_freq <- q1_rs_stmd_ac %>%
  count(Physical_Activity) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = round(n/sum(n)*100, 1)) %>%
  arrange(desc(n))

print(activity_freq)
```
